purpose: Provides a disciplined probability framework for evaluating multi-link causal chains like the fiscal doom loop, preventing narrative bias from treating sequential scenarios as high-probability
components: Joint probability computation (multiplicative for independent events)
Correlation adjustment (conditional probabilities for causally linked events)
Survivorship bias correction (accounting for non-crisis episodes in the denominator)
Reserve currency exceptionalism discount (level shift vs delay mechanism)
current_assessment: Doom loop at six independent links yields ~5% joint probability. Correlation adjustment raises to 15-30%. Reserve currency status reduces from IMF base rate 35-40% to 8-12% for disorderly adjustment. The narrative treatment systematically overstates probability by treating chains as sequential rather than computing joint probabilities.
limitations: Link probabilities themselves are subjective estimates
Correlation structure between links is poorly understood
The framework cannot distinguish between structural resilience and luck
Independence vs correlation of failures is the dominant uncertainty and is itself unestimatable
